title: ISE ERS / OpenAPI Brute-Force, Token Theft, and Anomalous API-Caller Detection

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 8.3.6 ŌĆö strong auth + brute-force protection.
Art.21(2)(b) ŌĆö incident handling on API access abuse.
AC-7 (Unsuccessful Logon Attempts) ŌĆö direct alignment.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Run a script that attempts 51 wrong-password ERS calls. UC-17.1.57 fires for that user with `count=51`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Successful API calls. UC-17.1.57 does not fire.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Administrative and Operational Audit* category ŌĆö codes `52001` (ERS / OpenAPI auth success), `52002` (ERS / OpenAPI auth failure), `52003` (ERS / OpenAPI authz denied), `52004` (ERS / OpenAPI rate-limit). Fields: `api_user`, `client_ip`, `endpoint_path`, `http_method`, `http_status`. CIM mapping: `Authentication.action`.
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (52002, 52003, 52004)
| stats count, dc(client_ip) as unique_ips, dc(endpoint_path) as unique_paths by api_user
| where count > 50 OR (unique_ips > 5 AND count > 20)
description: Detects ERS / OpenAPI brute-force attempts, token theft (one user from many IPs), and anomalous high-volume callers ŌĆö symptoms of compromised API credentials or misuse.
value: ISE's ERS/OpenAPI exposes the entire control plane. A compromised API token can be used to read endpoint inventory, modify auth policies, or push ANC actions. Detecting brute-force or anomalous use within 5 minutes is the primary defense.
implementation: Confirm *Administrative and Operational Audit* at INFO. Schedule every 5 min, search window `-15m@m to @m`, throttle on `api_user` for 30 min.
visualization: (1) Single value: API-failure events last 1h. (2) Top-N: API users by failure count. (3) Map: client_ip distribution.

references.title: Cisco ISE ŌĆö ERS API Configuration

knownFalsePositives: (a) **Splunk-side polling burst** ŌĆö Multiple Splunk modular inputs polling ISE simultaneously may briefly trigger; tag those service accounts. (b) **CMDB sync bursts** ŌĆö Periodic CMDB sync produces high-volume reads. (c) **First-day deployment of integration** ŌĆö Integration testing produces failure bursts.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó ISE ERS / OpenAPI enabled.
ŌĆó `api_users.csv` with `api_user`, `expected_caller_ip`, `expected_endpoint_paths`.

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
Enable *Administrative and Operational Audit* at INFO.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search
Search in `spl` field.

A secondary anomaly check matches `client_ip` against `api_users.csv` `expected_caller_ip`:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=52001 earliest=-1h
| lookup api_users.csv api_user OUTPUT expected_caller_ip
| where !match(client_ip, expected_caller_ip)
```

Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Provoke as above.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE ŌĆö API Access Audit":
ŌĆó Single value: failures last 1h.
ŌĆó Top-N users.
ŌĆó Map of caller IPs.

Alerting: PagerDuty high on brute-force; medium on caller-IP anomaly.

Step 5 ŌĆö Troubleshooting
ŌĆó **Bursts of 52004 (rate-limit)** ŌĆö Tune ISE rate-limit OR fix the offending integration's polling cadence.
ŌĆó **52002 from internal dev IP** ŌĆö Dev iterating on a script; coordinate.
ŌĆó **Persistent 52003 (authz denied)** ŌĆö User has wrong role; review.
grandmaExplanation: ISE has an interface for other systems to connect to it. We watch for too many wrong passwords or unusual patterns of use ŌĆö these can mean someone is trying to break in or has stolen a password.
